programme.desc: Filmmaker Barri Cohen leads part detective story, part social history as she uncovers the truth about Alfie and Louis, her two long-dead half-brothers. They were institutionalized at the Huronia Regional Centre in Orillia in the 1950s.

programme.title: The Nature of Things
programme.desc: The key element of life on Earth, it has the power to build and destroy.

programme.title: Schmeichel
programme.desc: Documentary profile of Peter Schmeichel, the goalkeeper who won the Euros with Denmark in 1992 and was part of Manchester United's 1999 treble-winning squad.

programme.title: Dettori
programme.desc: The story of arguably the world's greatest jockey, Frankie Dettori considered by many as the star of racing, and one of the most decorated sportsmen of all time.

programme.title: The Loneliest Whale: The Search for 52
programme.desc: Documentary about the search to find a rare whale that calls out at a frequency unrecognised by other whales. This mysterious creature is believed to have lived its life in complete solitude, pointing to a greater awareness of human loneliness than ever before.

programme.title: Discovering: Jamie Foxx
programme.desc: The story of the life and career of Academy Award-winning star Jamie Foxx, known for his roles in Ray, Dreamgirls, Collateral and Django Unchained.

programme.title: Discovering: Ryan Gosling
programme.desc: The story of the life and career of Ryan Gosling, known for his roles in The Notebook, La La Land, Drive, First Man and Barbie.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of actor-turned-director Clint Eastwood, known for Unforgiven, Million Dollar Baby and American Sniper.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of Taiwanese film-maker Ang Lee, known for Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, Brokeback Mountain and Life of Pi.

programme.title: The Kid Stays in the Picture
programme.desc: Film producer Robert Evans narrates this documentary chronicling the ups and downs of his extraordinary life. Among the topics covered are his big break into the movie industry, his failed marriage to Ali McGraw, his relationships with Grace Kelly and Ava Gardner, his descent into drug addiction, and his journey back to respectability. With contributions by friends and family, and including footag

programme.title: Stevie Van Zandt: Disciple
programme.desc: Tracing the musician and actor's career, including his work as a member of Bruce Springsteen's E Street Band and his role as Silvio Dante in The Sopranos.

programme.title: After the Bite
programme.desc: Documentary investigating the surge in great white sharks patrolling Cape Cod's shallow waters and the fallout from one young man's death.

programme.title: The Armstrong Lie
programme.desc: Documentary following cycling champion Lance Armstrong's fall from grace. Film-maker Alex Gibney was initially commissioned to follow the cyclist as he prepared to make a professional comeback by competing in the Tour de France, only to find himself documenting an unprecedented scandal as Armstrong was exposed as having used performance-enhancing drugs.

programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: God Bless Ozzy Osbourne
programme.desc: A profile of Black Sabbath member Ozzy Osbourne, from the wild and hedonistic days that adversely affected his personal life, through the years of depression, alcoholism and drug dependency that followed. The programme also reveals how he overcame his demons, with the help of his second wife Sharon. Featuring interviews with Paul McCartney, Motley Crue drummer Tommy Lee and others.

programme.title: Nick Cave's Veiled World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the creative universe, enduring influence and impact of the singer-songwriter and artist, whose legacy spans music, literature, film, and visual art. Contributors including musicians Florence Welch, Colin Greenwood and Warren Ellis, designer Bella Freud, film-maker Wim Wenders and writer Irvine Welsh discuss his influence on their own creative work.

programme.title: Wise Guy: David Chase and The Sopranos
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining the origins and development of the drama, as well as looking at the life and career of its creator and writer David Chase.

programme.title: A Cheetah's Pride
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary. This series follows a pair of warring cheetahs as they fight for possession of their cubs.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: In Zimbabwe, an African wild dog pack find themselves outnumbered by lions and hyenas, and the pups must learn quickly if the pack is to survive.

programme.title: The Island of Giants
programme.desc: Exploring how whale sharks drive the culture of Santa Maria, the only European hotspot for these giants and why warming seas may be drawing them in.

programme.title: Shadowlands
programme.desc: The wildlife of New Zealand's South Island, a brutal place hemmed in by giant mountains, wild winds and the rough Pacific Ocean. Rare penguins are locked in a daily game of cat and mouse with giant sea lions, which must also battle the elements to survive.

programme.title: Shadowlands
programme.desc: Winter's impact on the deep fjords and tidal lagoons of southwestern New Zealand. As bottlenose dolphins escape the freezing fjords, longfin eels are called back to sea.

programme.title: Shadowlands
programme.desc: The southeastern coast of New Zealand is where young marine animals become adults. Here, young whales, dolphins and seals strike out on their own adventures for the first time.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: In the wild, having company means protection, while isolation often leads to danger. The documentary reveals how groups of animals work together to fight off threats.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: Species that cannot survive in one place and must migrate, following epic flights, sea journeys and road trips that play a key part in the lives of many animals.

programme.title: Land of the Far North
programme.desc: Life in the Arctic Circle, where the sun hardly sets in summer. Food is abundant, but animals and plants alike face great challenges such as sleep deprivation, pests and predators.

programme.title: Land of the Far North
programme.desc: Cold and darkness dominate the Arctic Circle during winter, but nature copes astonishingly well. This documentary reveals how plants and animals survive these brutal conditions.

programme.title: Africa's Claws and Jaws
programme.desc: Sharks are among the planet's oldest and most highly evolved predators. This documentary follows the people dedicated to learning more about these creatures and their plight.

programme.title: Africa's Claws and Jaws
programme.desc: The seldom-seen reptiles that lurk in the African shadows. Masters of the underworld, these snakes and lizards have conquered almost every habitat there.

programme.title: Europe's Amazon
programme.desc: Following a 750km journey down the Drava river, starting with the current's Alpine origins to its delta in the Pannonian Basin.

programme.title: Europe's Amazon
programme.desc: Diving into the great floodplains that lie along the Drava River, areas that are at risk of intense seasonal flooding, before exploring the wetlands of Kopacki Rit.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: The creatures that live in the ocean's darkest depths and the skills they have built over the years, from blind worms who fish to crabs who climb trees and more.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: Discover some of the planet's strangest animals, from snapping shrimp to wolf eels, and see how these ocean-dwelling creatures ensure mutual survival.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: The bizarre mating rituals of the ocean's most peculiar creatures, from bloody battles to super-size litters.

programme.title: The Island of Giants
programme.desc: Exploring how whale sharks drive the culture of Santa Maria, the only European hotspot for these giants and why warming seas may be drawing them in.

programme.title: The Kimberley: Australia's Wild West
programme.desc: A look at Australia's Wild West - The Kimberley - the animals who live there and tracking the quests of those working to protect them.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is no

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while there's no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. However, the harvestman spider

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.desc: After years of hard work, a real estate agent uses the fruits of her labor to purchase an investment property that can be flipped for dual purposes; a new home for her daughter that will one day transition into an excellent rental property.

programme.desc: When an expat returns to her Georgia hometown to find a lakeside retreat for her and her three young sons, a longtime friend joins her to help find the right property and spearhead the renovations needed to transform it into her perfect cabin.
